trigger: Skipped skill prompts, wrote throwaway inline Plugin API code for 488 ops; correct but burned excessive tokens and bypassed tested tooling.
lesson: Always use the existing pipeline scripts (audit/resolve/bulk-update/token-lookup) — never write ad-hoc inline Plugin API code for tree walks or bulk writes.

trigger: Bound 4 corners with 4 individual setBoundVariable calls — slow.
lesson: setBoundVariable('cornerRadius', v) binds all 4 corners in one call — ~5x faster than per-corner.

trigger: Fixed setup cost per mcp_figma_use_figma call ~7.5s (getNodeByIdAsync ~1.8s + 4x parallel getVariableByIdAsync ~5.6s).
lesson: Amortise setup cost over as many nodes as possible in one use_figma call (308 nodes in one call/78s is fine). Avoid many small calls.
For NV-only writes with known IDs, skip getLocalVariablesAsync; use getVariableByIdAsync via Promise.all. Cache the name→id map per fileKey+version.

trigger: figma.getLocalEffectStyles() (sync) returned empty/stale in some Plugin API contexts, giving a false ∅ in QA.
lesson: Always use the async read APIs (getLocalEffectStylesAsync, getLocalVariablesAsync). Sync style/variable reads can be stale.

trigger: Needed to bind fills on icon VECTORs inside INSTANCE children.
lesson: setBoundVariableForPaint works on nested instance-override nodes — traverse comp.children recursively; no getNodeByIdAsync per node needed.

trigger: Ordering of textStyleId vs fill on TEXT nodes.
lesson: textStyleId assignment on TEXT nodes is synchronous and works on instance-override TEXT nodes; assign before fill to avoid ordering issues.

trigger: 'The user cancelled the tool call' was misread as a Figma/network hang.
lesson: A cancelled tool call means the user clicked Cancel in the approval dialog, not a network hang. Run a 2-line smoke test to prove connectivity before bulk writes.

trigger: Sync figma.getNodeById can fail to resolve inner node IDs mid-script.
lesson: Always use figma.getNodeByIdAsync; obtain inner node refs via tree traversal, never getNodeById in a loop.

trigger: Planning a future swap from the local .hermes store to a real Hermes install.
lesson: Real Hermes contracts: state=SessionDB (~/.hermes/state.db) with a state_meta k/v table — map state.write(runId) -> set_meta('run:<runId>', json), same pattern as goal:<id>. A run=kanban_db.Run (claim->complete|blocked|failed|timeout|reclaimed). The ODIN loop=goals.py Ralph loop (status active|paused|done|cleared, judge verdict done|continue). Skills(SKILL.md, loaded on demand) vs Memory(compact, injected). manifest.json=a skill-bundle {name,description,skills[],instruction}. Keep these analogies intact.

trigger: FDS-Card audit: Padding=True uses TS key `spacing` (maps to itemSpacing/gap) instead of `verticalPadding`/`horizontalPadding`; also uses generic fds-spacing-200 instead of the component-specific fds-spacing-const-container-card.
lesson: For card/container components: check that TS uses `verticalPadding`/`horizontalPadding` keys (not `spacing`) for padding insets. Prefer fds-spacing-const-container-card over fds-spacing-200 for card internal padding.

ruleProposal.diff: # Consider adding a rule: FDS-Card Padding=True -> key verticalPadding + horizontalPadding -> spacing.fds-spacing-const.container.fds-spacing-const-container-card

trigger: COMPONENT_SET with zero GROUPs and all COMPONENT children already VERTICAL AL.
lesson: When a COMPONENT_SET scan returns zero GROUPs and all COMPONENT children have non-NONE layoutMode, classify the run property-only: emit only sizing/slot-AL property corrections, no structural OPS. Note: Figma 2025+ SLOT nodes inside a COMPONENT are writable (unlike INSTANCE internals).

trigger: FDS-Card audit: TS-only draft, wrong border token on surface theme, generic padding token.
lesson: For card/container components: (1) padding insets should use verticalPadding/horizontalPadding keys, not `spacing` (which maps to itemSpacing); prefer fds-spacing-const-container-card over generic fds-spacing-200. (2) Watch for copy-paste border-token errors across themes (surface variants carrying fds-on-alternate-surface-ulow). (3) var.fds.* TS prefix signals NV binding is intended but may be unapplied (varMap empty).
